top_stories.title: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Ivanti flaw by Sunday
top_stories.summary: CISA issued Binding Operational Directive BOD 26-04 ordering federal agencies to patch an actively exploited Ivanti Sentry vulnerability within three days. Ivanti Sentry is an enterprise gateway product used for managing and securing mobile device access to backend systems, making this directly relevant to access management infrastructure.

top_stories.title: Oracle mitigates PeopleSoft zero-day exploited in data theft attacks
top_stories.summary: Oracle has disclosed a critical zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-35273) in PeopleSoft Suite that enables unauthenticated remote code execution. The flaw has been actively exploited by the ShinyHunters threat group in data theft attacks, prompting Oracle to issue a mitigation.

top_stories.title: Researchers release details, PoC for exploited Check Point VPN flaw (CVE-2026-50751)
top_stories.summary: WatchTowr researchers published a technical analysis and proof-of-concept "Detection Artefact Generator" for CVE-2026-50751, an authentication bypass vulnerability in Check Point's Remote Access VPN and Mobile Access software. Check Point patched the flaw on June 8, 2026, after confirming limited active exploitation, with broader opportunistic attacks now anticipated following public disclosure.

top_stories.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade
top_stories.summary: A China-linked threat group tracked as Velvet Ant backdoored Linux PAM (Pluggable Authentication Modules) and OpenSSH components to maintain persistent covert access for nearly a decade. The group targeted authentication infrastructure specifically to survive remediation efforts, with findings reported by security firm Sygnia.

top_stories.title: ServiceNow Flaw Exploited to Gain Unauthorized Access to Customer Instances
top_stories.summary: ServiceNow applied an emergency security update on June 5, 2026, after threat actors exploited a vulnerability to gain unauthorized access to customer instances. The flaw allowed unauthenticated users to obtain deeper access to susceptible hosted instances, with ServiceNow issuing an advisory to affected customers.

news.title: phpBB forum fixes auth bypass bug lurking for a decade
news.summary: phpBB forum software patched a 10-year-old authentication bypass vulnerability that allowed attackers to log in as any user, including administrators. The flaw represents a critical authentication control failure affecting any organization running phpBB forums.

news.title: How to use NIST and ISO frameworks to govern AI agents
news.summary: The article examines how NIST and ISO frameworks can be applied to govern AI agents operating autonomously in enterprise environments. It focuses on the IAM-relevant challenge of AI agents carrying delegated authority, accessing sensitive systems, and invoking internal APIs without direct human oversight.

news.title: ShinyHunters Exploits Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day (CVE-2026-35273) to Breach Universities
news.summary: ShinyHunters (tracked as UNC6240 by Mandiant) exploited a zero-day vulnerability in Oracle PeopleSoft (CVE-2026-35273) to breach enterprise systems, primarily targeting universities, between May 27 and June 9. Oracle did not publish its advisory until June 10, meaning the attacks occurred before a patch was available.

news.title: IDMExpress Announces 24×7 Managed Services for Leading IAM/PAM Solutions with Innovative Unified Pricing Model
news.summary: IDMExpress announced a 24×7 Managed Identity Security Operations service covering IAM, PAM, and PKI platforms. The service uses a unified pricing model with a flexible bucket of hours across supported platforms, with no vendor lock-in.

news.title: Enterprise Identities Are Under Siege. Josys Is Changing How Organizations Fight Back.
news.summary: Josys announced new AI-native platform capabilities aimed at addressing enterprise identity challenges including credential theft, AI agent governance, and SaaS access management. The announcement targets three areas: credential theft prevention, governance of AI agents, and access policies for SaaS environments.

news.title: Not all AIs are equal so CIOs need to prioritize actions when assessing risks
news.summary: The article addresses how CIOs should approach identity governance and risk-based security controls as AI agents proliferate. It frames AI agent management as an identity governance challenge requiring prioritized risk assessment.

news.title: Threat actors are recruiting the people who hold cloud logins
news.summary: Threat actors are actively recruiting insiders who hold cloud platform credentials to gain unauthorized access to corporate data and applications. The cybercrime underground has developed systematic methods to target and compromise employees with privileged cloud logins, turning credential holders into security vulnerabilities.

news.title: Acsense Achieves FedRAMP Certification, Extending IAM Resilience to Federal Agencies
news.summary: Acsense has achieved FedRAMP certification, enabling federal agencies to access its IAM resilience platform through Knox Systems. The certification covers purpose-built protection, recovery, and continuous compliance validation for Okta and Microsoft Entra ID environments.

news.title: Netwrix 2026 Data and Identity Security Report: AI Adoption Outpacing AI Readiness, Driving a 4x Breach Gap
news.summary: Netwrix released its 2026 Data and Identity Security Report, finding that AI adoption is significantly outpacing AI security readiness across organizations. Key findings include that only 11% of organizations report full AI security readiness, 17% remain entirely unprepared, and 45% are still developing governance programs, resulting in a 4x breach gap between prepared and unprepared organizations.

news.title: Delinea Recognized as an Overall Leader in the 2026 KuppingerCole Leadership Compass™ for Privileged Access Management
news.summary: Delinea has been recognized as an Overall Leader in the KuppingerCole Leadership Compass™ for Privileged Access Management (PAM) 2026. This analyst recognition places Delinea among the top-ranked vendors in the PAM market according to KuppingerCole's evaluation framework.

news.title: Oasis Security Announces Integration with Zscaler to Extend Zero Trust to Non-Human and Agentic Identities
news.summary: Oasis Security has announced a new integration with Zscaler that extends zero trust principles to non-human identities and AI agents. The integration combines Oasis Security's identity governance capabilities with inline enforcement through Zscaler's Zero Trust Exchange, creating a unified control plane for managing non-human and agentic identities.

news.title: Rubrik launches Autonomous Business Recovery to rebuild cloud applications after cyberattacks
news.summary: Rubrik has launched Autonomous Business Recovery (ABR) for Cloud Applications, an agentic cyber resilience solution that recovers cloud applications including data, network, identity, and configurations after cyberattacks. Identity recovery is one component of a broader backup and disaster recovery product rather than the primary focus of the solution.

news.title: SailPoint shares fall despite earnings beat and raised guidance
news.summary: SailPoint shares dropped more than 11% despite the identity security company beating analyst expectations on revenue and adjusted earnings while also raising its guidance. The selloff suggests investor expectations were set higher than the reported results.

news.title: Amazon S3 Access Grants are now available in the AWS European Sovereign Cloud (Germany) Region
news.summary: Amazon S3 Access Grants are now available in the AWS European Sovereign Cloud (Germany) Region. The feature maps identities from directories such as Microsoft Entra ID or AWS IAM principals to S3 datasets for access control purposes.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant chatbot to bypass authentication and reset passwords, successfully seizing high-profile Instagram accounts including the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force Chief Master Sergeant. Instructions for the attack were distributed via Telegram, demonstrating a social engineering vulnerability in AI-powered account recovery workflows.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, prompting Congressional inquiries from both chambers. CISA is actively working to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor publicly exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud account credentials and internal system access details in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked repository contained files detailing CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes, representing a significant credential and secrets management failure.
